input: Build me a Telegram bot agent called support-bot
expected_behavior: Scaffolds a Node.js project with src/tools/, installs grammy + groq-sdk + better-sqlite3, creates all 12 source files in dependency-safe order (logger → config → security → tokens → memory → registry → tool → delegate → llm → agent → bot → index)

input: Create an HTTP agent called api-guard with all 3 security checkpoints
expected_behavior: Generates security.ts with sanitizeInput (CP1), buildSecurePrompt (CP2), and validateOutput (CP3). Each checkpoint logs but does not block. Regex patterns cover 8+ injection patterns.

input: Build a Discord agent with proper token budget management
expected_behavior: Creates tokens.ts with estimation via Math.ceil(text.length/4), budget calculation (modelContext - maxTokens - systemPrompt - 10% margin), and oldest-first trimming that never drops the most recent user message.
criteria: budget recalculated before each LLM call

input: Create a Telegram agent with sub-agent delegation support
expected_behavior: Generates delegate.ts with sub-agent definitions, registry.ts with dynamic executor slot, and agent.ts that calls initDelegation() at startup. Depth=1 limit enforced — sub-agents cannot delegate.

input: Build an agent with Groq primary and OpenRouter fallback
expected_behavior: Creates llm.ts with Groq as primary provider, OpenRouter as automatic fallback on rate limit or timeout (30s). Provider switch is logged. Both providers use the same tool-calling interface.
